notes: Batons, particularly expandable types, are generally prohibited weapons in Australia and cannot be carried by civilians.
Kubotans may be legal as simple keychains but weaponised versions can be prohibited
Self-defence keychains may be legal if they are ordinary items, but weaponised designs can be illegal.
Carrying a sword in public is generally restricted in Australia unless there is a lawful reason such as work or events.
Tasers are classified as prohibited weapons in Australia and are not legal for civilian ownership.
Pepper spray is illegal in most Australian states with limited exceptions
Switchblades are prohibited knives in Australia and are illegal to possess or import.
Butterfly knives are banned in Australia and cannot be legally owned or carried.
Stun guns are prohibited weapons across Australia and cannot be legally owned by civilians.
Brass knuckles are prohibited weapons throughout Australia and cannot be legally owned or carried.
